hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in TM5 (residue 205/208/209/212), while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity with SSTR2 ECL2 (residue 192/193/195/197) and avoid off-target SSTR3/5 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong ddG binding but are structurally suboptimal. Tightening structural constraints and increasing candidate diversity are necessary to improve pass rates and maintain selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Increasing the number of candidates can improve the chances of generating structurally valid designs with better pLDDT scores.
Reducing the clash threshold will enforce better packing and reduce structural artifacts, which are likely contributing to low pLDDT scores.
